goals.title: Multi-Project Chat Sessions
goals.description: Enable conversational chat with persistent sessions bound to workspaces
goals.success_criteria: Users can create, resume, and fork sessions per project with full message history
goals.verification_method: E2E test: create session, send messages, close, resume, verify history intact

goals.title: Provider-Agnostic Agent Loop
goals.description: Support multiple LLM providers (OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic) with unified interface
goals.success_criteria: Users can switch between providers mid-session; tool schemas translate correctly
goals.verification_method: Integration test: same prompt produces valid responses from each provider

goals.title: Tool-Use via MCP with Approvals
goals.description: Execute filesystem, shell, and git operations through MCP with explicit user approvals and audit logging
goals.success_criteria: All write/exec operations require approval; audit logs capture all tool calls with redacted secrets
goals.verification_method: E2E test: trigger tool call, verify approval prompt, check audit.db entry

goals.title: Auto-Context Injection
goals.description: Automatically inject project context (INTENT.md, tasks.json, logs) into each turn
goals.success_criteria: Agent receives truncated context without exceeding token limits; context reflects current project state
goals.verification_method: Unit test: verify context builder includes expected files and respects token budget

goals.title: Meta Self-Healing
goals.description: Agent can locate, edit, build, and request restart of the Orchestrator itself
goals.success_criteria: Demo: agent edits orchestrator file, builds binary, requests restart successfully
goals.verification_method: E2E test: agent modifies test file in orchestrator, triggers build, verifies change

stories.title: SQLite Sessions Database Schema
stories.description: As a developer, I want a SQLite database schema for sessions, messages, and tool_calls so that conversation state persists across restarts
acceptance_criteria: Database file created at configurable path
Tables: sessions, messages, tool_calls with proper foreign keys
Indexes on session_id and created_at for query performance
stories.contract: SessionsDB: create_session(project_path, provider, model) -> session_id; get_session(id) -> Session; add_message(session_id, role, content) -> message_id; get_messages(session_id) -> List[Message]; log_tool_call(session_id, tool_name, input, output, approved) -> tool_call_id

tasks.title: Define Database Schema Types
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for Session, Message, and ToolCall entities
tasks.technical_strategy: Use Pydantic BaseModel with Field validators. Import Optional, List from typing. Define enums for MessageRole (user/assistant/system/tool) and ToolCallStatus (pending/approved/rejected/executed).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.db.models import Session, Message, ToolCall; print(Session.model_json_schema())'

tasks.title: Implement SQLite Repository
tasks.description: Create SessionsRepository class with CRUD operations and migrations
tasks.technical_strategy: Use sqlite3 stdlib with context managers for connections. Implement schema_version table for migrations. Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Store JSON fields as TEXT with json.dumps/loads.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.db.repository import SessionsRepository; r = SessionsRepository(":memory:"); r.initialize(); print("OK")'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Sessions Repository
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for all repository operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest with fixtures for in-memory DB. Test create/read/update/delete for all entities. Test foreign key constraints and index usage with EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN.

stories.title: Provider Adapter Interface
stories.description: As a developer, I want a provider-agnostic interface for LLM calls so that I can swap providers without changing business logic
acceptance_criteria: Abstract base class defines chat_completion and stream_completion methods
Unified response format regardless of provider
stories.contract: ProviderAdapter: chat_completion(messages, tools, model) -> CompletionResponse; stream_completion(messages, tools, model) -> AsyncIterator[StreamChunk]; translate_tools(tools) -> ProviderToolSchema; count_tokens(text) -> int

tasks.title: Define Provider Adapter ABC and Response Types
tasks.description: Create abstract base class and unified response types
tasks.technical_strategy: Use abc.ABC with @abstractmethod decorators. Define CompletionResponse, StreamChunk, ToolCall as Pydantic models. Include usage stats (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_cost).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.base import ProviderAdapter, CompletionResponse; print("OK")'

tasks.title: Implement Tool Schema Translator Base
tasks.description: Create base tool schema translation with provider-specific overrides
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool dataclass matching MCP spec. Create translate_to_openai, translate_to_anthropic, translate_to_gemini abstract methods. Handle parameter type mapping (string/number/boolean/object/array).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.tools import MCPTool, ToolSchemaTranslator; print("OK")'

stories.title: OpenAI Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-4o) so that I can leverage OpenAI's capabilities
acceptance_criteria: Streaming responses work with tool calls
Proper error handling for rate limits and API errors
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider; p = OpenAIProvider(); print(p.available_models())'

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI Provider Class
tasks.description: Create OpenAIProvider implementing ProviderAdapter
tasks.technical_strategy: Use openai>=1.0 AsyncOpenAI client. Import tiktoken for token counting (cl100k_base for GPT-4). Handle tool_calls in streaming via delta accumulation. Map finish_reason to unified enum.

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI Tool Schema Translation
tasks.description: Translate MCP tools to OpenAI function calling format
tasks.technical_strategy: Map MCPTool to OpenAI's tools format with type='function'. Handle required vs optional parameters. Include strict=True for JSON mode compatibility.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider; p = OpenAIProvider(); print(p.translate_tools([]))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for OpenAI Provider
tasks.description: Write unit tests with mocked API responses
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest-asyncio and respx or httpx mock. Test streaming accumulation, tool call parsing, error handling (RateLimitError, APIError). Mock tiktoken to avoid encoding overhead in tests.

stories.title: Anthropic Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use Anthropic models (Claude) so that I can leverage Claude's capabilities
acceptance_criteria: Streaming responses work with tool use
Proper error handling for rate limits
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.anthropic import AnthropicProvider; p = AnthropicProvider(); print(p.available_models())'

tasks.title: Implement Anthropic Provider Class
tasks.description: Create AnthropicProvider implementing ProviderAdapter
tasks.technical_strategy: Use anthropic>=0.18 AsyncAnthropic client. Handle tool_use content blocks in streaming. Map stop_reason to unified enum. Approximate tokens as len(text)/4 or use anthropic.count_tokens if available.

tasks.title: Implement Anthropic Tool Schema Translation
tasks.description: Translate MCP tools to Anthropic tool_use format
tasks.technical_strategy: Map MCPTool to Anthropic's tools format with input_schema. Handle cache_control for prompt caching. Include tool_choice parameter support.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.anthropic import AnthropicProvider; p = AnthropicProvider(); print(p.translate_tools([]))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Anthropic Provider
tasks.description: Write unit tests with mocked API responses
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest-asyncio and respx. Test streaming content_block_delta events, tool_use block parsing. Mock rate limit responses (429) and verify retry logic.

stories.title: Gemini Provider Driver
stories.description: As a user, I want to use Google Gemini models so that I can leverage Gemini's capabilities
acceptance_criteria: Streaming responses work with function calling
Token counting uses Gemini's count_tokens API
Proper error handling for quota limits
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.gemini import GeminiProvider; p = GeminiProvider(); print(p.available_models())'

tasks.title: Implement Gemini Provider Class
tasks.description: Create GeminiProvider implementing ProviderAdapter
tasks.technical_strategy: Use google-generativeai>=0.4 with GenerativeModel. Handle function_call parts in streaming. Map finish_reason (STOP, MAX_TOKENS, SAFETY) to unified enum. Use model.count_tokens() for accurate counting.

tasks.title: Implement Gemini Tool Schema Translation
tasks.description: Translate MCP tools to Gemini function declarations
tasks.technical_strategy: Map MCPTool to FunctionDeclaration with parameters as Schema objects. Handle OBJECT type with properties. Include function_calling_config for mode control (AUTO, ANY, NONE).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.gemini import GeminiProvider; p = GeminiProvider(); print(p.translate_tools([]))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Gemini Provider
tasks.description: Write unit tests with mocked API responses
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest-asyncio and unittest.mock.patch on GenerativeModel. Test streaming chunk parsing, function_call extraction. Mock quota exceeded responses (429/503).

stories.title: Provider Registry and Model Picker
stories.description: As a user, I want to select and switch between providers/models so that I can use the best model for my task
acceptance_criteria: Registry lists all available providers and their models
Model picker validates provider API key availability
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.providers.registry import ProviderRegistry; r = ProviderRegistry(); print(r.list_providers())'
stories.contract: ProviderRegistry: list_providers() -> List[str]; list_models(provider) -> List[ModelInfo]; get_provider(name) -> ProviderAdapter; validate_credentials(provider) -> bool

tasks.title: Implement Provider Registry
tasks.description: Create singleton registry managing all providers
tasks.technical_strategy: Use class-level dict for provider registration. Lazy-load providers to avoid import errors when SDK not installed. Check env vars (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY) for availability.

tasks.title: Create ModelInfo Schema and Pricing Data
tasks.description: Define model metadata including pricing and context limits
tasks.technical_strategy: Create ModelInfo Pydantic model with fields: id, provider, context_window, input_price_per_1k, output_price_per_1k, supports_tools, supports_vision. Load pricing from embedded JSON or config.

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Provider Registry
tasks.description: Write tests for registry operations
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock environment variables using monkeypatch. Test provider availability detection, model listing, credential validation. Test error cases (missing SDK, invalid key).

stories.title: MCP Tool: read_file
stories.description: As an agent, I want to read file contents so that I can understand project code and configuration
acceptance_criteria: Supports text and binary files (base64 for binary)
Audit log entry created for each read
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.tools.filesystem import read_file; import asyncio; print(asyncio.run(read_file("README.md")))'
stories.contract: read_file(path: str, encoding: str = 'utf-8') -> FileContent(content: str, size: int, modified: datetime, is_binary: bool)

tasks.title: Define MCP Tool Schema for read_file
tasks.description: Create tool definition and response types
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool with name='read_file', description, and parameters schema. Create FileContent Pydantic model. Include error codes: PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE, FILE_NOT_FOUND, PERMISSION_DENIED.

tasks.title: Implement Path Validation Utility
tasks.description: Create secure path validation that prevents directory traversal
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pathlib.Path.resolve() to canonicalize. Check that resolved path starts with WORKSPACES_ROOT. Reject symlinks pointing outside workspace. Raise PathSecurityError for violations.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.tools.security import validate_path; validate_path("/tmp/test"); print("OK")'

tasks.title: Implement read_file Handler
tasks.description: Create async handler with audit logging
tasks.technical_strategy: Use aiofiles for async I/O. Detect binary via magic bytes or extension. Encode binary as base64. Log to audit.db via SessionsRepository.log_tool_call(). Handle encoding errors gracefully.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.tools.filesystem import read_file; import asyncio; asyncio.run(read_file("setup.py"))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for read_file
tasks.description: Write comprehensive tests including security cases
tasks.technical_strategy: Use tmp_path fixture for test files. Test path traversal attacks (../../../etc/passwd). Test binary detection, encoding handling. Verify audit log entries created.

stories.title: MCP Tool: write_file
stories.description: As an agent, I want to write file contents so that I can create and modify project files
acceptance_criteria: Requires explicit user approval before write
Creates backup before overwriting existing files
Audit log captures before/after content hashes
stories.contract: write_file(path: str, content: str, create_backup: bool = True) -> WriteResult(success: bool, backup_path: Optional[str], bytes_written: int)

tasks.title: Define MCP Tool Schema for write_file
tasks.description: Create tool definition with approval requirement
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool with requires_approval=True. Include create_backup parameter. Define WriteResult model. Error codes: PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE, PARENT_DIR_NOT_FOUND, DISK_FULL.

tasks.title: Implement Backup Manager
tasks.description: Create utility for file backups with rotation
tasks.technical_strategy: Store backups in .openexec/backups/{date}/{filename}.{timestamp}. Implement max_backups_per_file config (default 10). Use shutil.copy2 to preserve metadata. Return backup path for potential rollback.

tasks.title: Implement write_file Handler
tasks.description: Create async handler with backup and audit
tasks.technical_strategy: Check approval status before write. Create parent dirs with exist_ok=True. Compute SHA256 hash of old and new content for audit. Use atomic write (write to temp, then rename).

tasks.title: Unit Tests for write_file
tasks.description: Write tests including approval flow
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock approval system to test both approved/rejected cases. Verify backup creation. Test atomic write by simulating crash mid-write. Check audit log content hashes.

stories.title: MCP Tool: run_shell_command
stories.description: As an agent, I want to execute shell commands so that I can run builds, tests, and other CLI operations
acceptance_criteria: Commands execute within project working directory
Requires explicit user approval before execution
Captures stdout, stderr, and exit code
Secrets in output are redacted in audit log
stories.contract: run_shell_command(command: str, cwd: Optional[str], timeout: int = 300, env: Optional[Dict]) -> ShellResult(exit_code: int, stdout: str, stderr: str, duration_ms: int)

tasks.title: Define MCP Tool Schema for run_shell_command
tasks.description: Create tool definition with safety parameters
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool with requires_approval=True. Include timeout (default 300s, max 3600s), cwd (must be within workspace), env (merged with process env). Error codes: COMMAND_TIMEOUT, WORKSPACE_VIOLATION.

tasks.title: Implement Secret Redaction Utility
tasks.description: Create utility to detect and redact secrets in output
tasks.technical_strategy: Detect patterns: API keys (sk-*, xox-*, ghp_*), tokens (Bearer *), passwords (password=*, secret=*). Replace with [REDACTED]. Support custom patterns via config. Use regex with word boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement run_shell_command Handler
tasks.description: Create async handler with streaming output capture
tasks.technical_strategy: Use asyncio.create_subprocess_shell with PIPE for stdout/stderr. Stream output in real-time via StreamReader. Implement timeout with process.kill(). Redact before audit log, preserve original for user display.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.tools.shell import run_shell_command; import asyncio; print(asyncio.run(run_shell_command("echo hello")))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for run_shell_command
tasks.description: Write tests including timeout and redaction
tasks.technical_strategy: Test successful command, failed command (exit code), timeout (use sleep). Verify redaction patterns. Test cwd validation. Mock approval system.

stories.title: MCP Tool: git_apply_patch
stories.description: As an agent, I want to apply git patches so that I can make atomic code changes with rollback capability
acceptance_criteria: Applies unified diff patches via git apply
Requires explicit user approval with patch preview
Audit log captures full patch content
stories.contract: git_apply_patch(patch: str, check_only: bool = False) -> PatchResult(success: bool, files_changed: List[str], error: Optional[str])

tasks.title: Define MCP Tool Schema for git_apply_patch
tasks.description: Create tool definition with preview mode
tasks.technical_strategy: Define MCPTool with requires_approval=True. Include check_only parameter for dry-run. Parse patch to extract file list for preview. Error codes: INVALID_PATCH, CONFLICT, NOT_GIT_REPO.

tasks.title: Implement Patch Parser and Validator
tasks.description: Create utility to parse and validate unified diff format
tasks.technical_strategy: Parse --- and +++ lines for file paths. Validate @@ hunk headers. Check line counts match. Detect binary patches. Return structured PatchInfo with files_modified, lines_added, lines_removed.
tasks.verification_script:
python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.tools.git import parse_patch; print(parse_patch("--- a/test.py
+++ b/test.py
@@ -1 +1 @@
-old
+new"))'

tasks.title: Implement git_apply_patch Handler
tasks.description: Create handler using git apply subprocess
tasks.technical_strategy: Use git apply --check for validation. Use git apply --verbose for actual apply. Capture stderr for conflict details. Store patch in .openexec/patches/{timestamp}.patch for rollback reference.

tasks.title: Unit Tests for git_apply_patch
tasks.description: Write tests with real git repository
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest tmp_path with git init. Create test file, generate patch, apply, verify. Test conflict detection. Test rollback via --reverse. Mock approval system.

stories.title: Tool Approval System
stories.description: As a user, I want to approve or reject tool calls so that I maintain control over agent actions
acceptance_criteria: Pending approvals displayed with full context
Approval history searchable in audit log
stories.contract: ApprovalManager: request_approval(tool_call_id, tool_name, params, context) -> ApprovalRequest; await_approval(request_id, timeout) -> ApprovalResult; approve(request_id) -> None; reject(request_id, reason) -> None

tasks.title: Define Approval Data Models
tasks.description: Create schemas for approval requests and responses
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ApprovalRequest with id, tool_name, params, context_summary, created_at, expires_at. Define ApprovalResult enum (APPROVED, REJECTED, TIMEOUT). Store in sessions DB approval_requests table.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.approval.models import ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResult; print(ApprovalRequest.model_fields)'

tasks.title: Implement ApprovalManager
tasks.description: Create async manager with timeout handling
tasks.technical_strategy: Use asyncio.Event for await_approval synchronization. Store pending approvals in dict with cleanup on timeout. Emit WebSocket events for UI notification. Support bulk_approve by tool pattern.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.approval import ApprovalManager; am = ApprovalManager(); print(am.pending_count())'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for ApprovalManager
tasks.description: Write tests for approval flow and timeout
tasks.technical_strategy: Use pytest-asyncio. Test approval/reject paths. Test timeout expiration with short timeout. Test concurrent approval requests. Verify DB state after each action.

stories.title: Audit Logging System
stories.description: As an administrator, I want comprehensive audit logs so that I can review all agent actions and tool executions
acceptance_criteria: All tool calls logged with input/output/timing
Logs queryable by session, tool, time range
stories.contract: AuditLogger: log_tool_call(session_id, tool_name, input, output, duration_ms, approved) -> log_id; query(filters) -> List[AuditEntry]; export(format, filters) -> bytes

tasks.title: Define Audit Entry Schema
tasks.description: Create comprehensive audit entry model
tasks.technical_strategy: Define AuditEntry with: id, session_id, timestamp, tool_name, input_hash, output_hash, input_redacted, output_redacted, duration_ms, approval_status, error. Use SHA256 for content hashes.

tasks.title: Implement AuditLogger
tasks.description: Create logger with redaction and retention
tasks.technical_strategy: Integrate with SessionsRepository. Apply redact_secrets before storage. Implement query builder with filters. Support pagination with offset/limit. Background task for retention cleanup.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.audit import AuditLogger; al = AuditLogger(); print(al.query(limit=10))'

tasks.title: Implement Export Functionality
tasks.description: Create JSON and CSV export handlers
tasks.technical_strategy: Use json.dumps for JSON export with datetime serialization. Use csv.DictWriter for CSV. Support streaming for large exports. Include metadata header with export timestamp and filters.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.audit import AuditLogger; print(AuditLogger().export("json", limit=1))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Audit System
tasks.description: Write comprehensive tests for logging and export
tasks.technical_strategy: Test redaction applied correctly. Test query filters (by session, tool, date range). Test export formats. Test retention cleanup. Use in-memory DB for isolation.

stories.title: Auto-Context Builder
stories.description: As an agent, I want automatic context injection so that I understand the project state without explicit requests
acceptance_criteria: INTENT.md content injected into system prompt
stories.contract: ContextBuilder: build_context(project_path, token_budget) -> ProjectContext(intent: str, tasks_summary: str, recent_logs: str, total_tokens: int)

tasks.title: Define Context Schema
tasks.description: Create ProjectContext and component schemas
tasks.technical_strategy: Define ProjectContext Pydantic model with intent, tasks_summary, recent_logs, file_summaries, total_tokens. Include priority weights for truncation decisions.

tasks.title: Implement Context Gatherers
tasks.description: Create individual gatherers for each context source
tasks.technical_strategy: IntentGatherer: read .openexec/INTENT.md or INTENT.md. TasksGatherer: parse tasks.json, summarize by status. LogsGatherer: tail .openexec/logs/*.log. Each returns (content, token_count).

tasks.title: Implement Context Builder with Token Budgeting
tasks.description: Create builder that prioritizes and truncates context
tasks.technical_strategy: Priority order: intent (40%), tasks (30%), logs (20%), files (10%). Truncate lowest priority first. Use tiktoken for accurate counting. Return early if budget exceeded.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.context import ContextBuilder; cb = ContextBuilder(); print(cb.build_context("/tmp", 4000))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Context Builder
tasks.description: Write tests for gathering and truncation
tasks.technical_strategy: Create test project with INTENT.md, tasks.json, log files. Test each gatherer independently. Test budget enforcement with various limits. Test missing file handling.

stories.title: Agent Loop with Tool Execution
stories.description: As a developer, I want a complete agent loop so that the LLM can reason and execute tools iteratively
acceptance_criteria: Loop continues until no tool calls or max iterations
Tool results fed back to LLM
Approval requests pause loop until resolved
stories.contract: AgentLoop: run(session_id, user_message) -> AsyncIterator[LoopEvent]; LoopEvent = AssistantMessage | ToolCall | ToolResult | ApprovalRequired | Error | Done

tasks.title: Define Loop Event Types
tasks.description: Create event types for loop progress reporting
tasks.technical_strategy: Use Union type for LoopEvent. Define AssistantMessage(content, is_final), ToolCall(id, name, params), ToolResult(tool_call_id, result), ApprovalRequired(request_id), Error(message), Done(summary).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.loop.events import LoopEvent, AssistantMessage; print(AssistantMessage.__doc__)'

tasks.title: Implement Tool Executor
tasks.description: Create executor that routes tool calls to handlers
tasks.technical_strategy: Registry of tool_name -> handler_function. Check requires_approval before execution. Await approval if needed. Catch handler exceptions, convert to ToolError. Timeout individual tool calls.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.loop.executor import ToolExecutor; te = ToolExecutor(); print(te.available_tools())'

tasks.title: Implement Agent Loop Core
tasks.description: Create main loop with iteration control
tasks.technical_strategy: Use async generator (yield events). Build context via ContextBuilder. Call provider.stream_completion. Parse tool_calls from response. Execute tools, append results to messages. Limit to max_iterations (default 10).
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.loop import AgentLoop; print(AgentLoop(None, None).__doc__)'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Agent Loop
tasks.description: Write tests with mocked provider and tools
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock provider to return scripted responses with tool_calls. Mock tool handlers. Test iteration limit. Test approval pause/resume. Test error handling and recovery.

stories.title: Chat UI with Session Management
stories.description: As a user, I want a chat interface so that I can interact with the agent conversationally
acceptance_criteria: Chat input with send button and keyboard shortcut
Message history displayed with role indicators
stories.contract: WebSocket /ws/chat/{session_id}: send {type: 'message', content: str}; receive LoopEvent stream

tasks.title: Design UI Component Structure
tasks.description: Create component hierarchy and props interfaces
tasks.technical_strategy: Components: ChatContainer, MessageList, MessageBubble, ChatInput, SessionSidebar. Use TypeScript interfaces for all props. Follow existing claudecodeui patterns.

tasks.title: Implement WebSocket Client
tasks.description: Create WebSocket hook for real-time communication
tasks.technical_strategy: useWebSocket hook with auto-reconnect. Parse LoopEvent JSON. Buffer events during disconnect. Expose send function and events stream. Handle connection state.

tasks.title: Implement Chat Components
tasks.description: Build chat UI with streaming support
tasks.technical_strategy: Use react-markdown for rendering with syntax highlighting (prismjs). Implement typing indicator during streaming. Auto-scroll to bottom. Support code block copy.

tasks.title: Implement Session Sidebar
tasks.description: Build session management UI
tasks.technical_strategy: Fetch sessions via REST API. Display session title (first message preview). Support search/filter. Implement create, delete, fork actions. Show active session indicator.

stories.title: Model Picker UI
stories.description: As a user, I want to select and switch models so that I can use different providers for different tasks
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown shows available providers and models
Unavailable providers (missing API key) are grayed out
Model info tooltip shows pricing and context window

tasks.title: Create ModelPicker Component
tasks.description: Build dropdown with provider grouping
tasks.technical_strategy: Use Radix UI Select component. Group options by provider. Include model icon/logo. Show context window and pricing in option description. Disable unavailable options.

tasks.title: Implement Provider Availability Check
tasks.description: Create API endpoint and client hook
tasks.technical_strategy: GET /api/providers returns {provider: {available: bool, models: []}}. useSWR for caching. Show setup instructions for unavailable providers in tooltip.

tasks.title: Unit Tests for ModelPicker
tasks.description: Write tests for selection and availability
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock API responses for available/unavailable providers. Test selection updates session. Test mid-session switch confirmation dialog. Test keyboard navigation.

stories.title: Patch Preview and Apply UI
stories.description: As a user, I want to preview patches before applying so that I can verify changes

tasks.title: Create DiffViewer Component
tasks.description: Build syntax-highlighted diff display
tasks.technical_strategy: Use react-diff-viewer-continued or similar. Support unified/split modes. Apply language-specific syntax highlighting. Show line numbers and hunk headers.

tasks.title: Implement Patch Preview Modal
tasks.description: Create modal with apply/reject actions
tasks.technical_strategy: Display on ApprovalRequired event for git_apply_patch. Parse patch to show file tree. Integrate DiffViewer. Connect approve/reject to ApprovalManager API.

tasks.title: Implement Rollback UI
tasks.description: Build rollback history and actions
tasks.technical_strategy: List recent patches from audit log. Show rollback button for each. Call git_apply_patch with reverse=true. Confirm before rollback. Show success/failure toast.

stories.title: Cost and Token Dashboard
stories.description: As a user, I want to see token usage and costs so that I can manage my API spending
acceptance_criteria: Estimated cost based on model pricing

tasks.title: Create Usage Summary Component
tasks.description: Build token and cost display widget
tasks.technical_strategy: Display prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_cost. Use number formatting (K, M suffixes). Show per-turn breakdown in expandable section. Color-code by threshold.

tasks.title: Implement Usage API Endpoints
tasks.description: Create backend endpoints for usage stats
tasks.technical_strategy: GET /api/usage/summary?session_id returns aggregated stats. GET /api/usage/history?period=daily returns time series. Aggregate from audit log entries.

tasks.title: Create Usage Charts
tasks.description: Build visualization for usage trends
tasks.technical_strategy: Use recharts or similar. Line chart for token usage over time. Bar chart for cost by provider. Pie chart for model distribution. Support date range selection.

tasks.title: Implement Budget Alerts
tasks.description: Create budget configuration and alerting
tasks.technical_strategy: Settings page for budget limits (daily, monthly). Store in user preferences. Check budget on each API call. Show warning banner at 80%, block at 100%. Email notification option.

stories.title: Session History Summarization
stories.description: As a user, I want long conversations summarized so that context stays within limits
acceptance_criteria: Summary preserves key decisions and tool results
stories.contract: SessionSummarizer: summarize_session(session_id, max_tokens) -> SummaryResult(summary: str, messages_summarized: int, tokens_saved: int)

tasks.title: Design Summarization Strategy
tasks.description: Define rules for what to preserve vs summarize
tasks.technical_strategy: Preserve: tool call results with files modified, errors, user decisions. Summarize: exploratory questions, repeated explanations. Use sliding window: keep last N turns verbatim, summarize older.

tasks.title: Implement SessionSummarizer
tasks.description: Create summarizer using LLM
tasks.technical_strategy: System prompt: 'Summarize conversation preserving: file changes, errors, decisions, key context. Output as structured bullet points.' Use cheap model (haiku/flash). Store summary as system message.

tasks.title: Integrate Summarization into Agent Loop
tasks.description: Add auto-summarization trigger
tasks.technical_strategy: Check context size before each LLM call. If exceeds threshold (e.g., 80% of context window), trigger summarization. Insert summary, mark old messages as summarized (keep in DB).

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Summarization
tasks.description: Write tests for summarization logic
tasks.technical_strategy: Test preservation rules with various message types. Test token counting accuracy. Test summary quality assertions (must contain file paths, error messages). Mock LLM responses.

stories.title: Session Fork Capability
stories.description: As a user, I want to fork sessions so that I can explore alternative approaches
acceptance_criteria: Fork creates new session with copied history
Fork point clearly marked in both sessions
Forked session can use different model
Session tree view shows fork relationships
stories.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.db.repository import SessionsRepository; r = SessionsRepository(":memory:"); r.initialize(); print(r.fork_session)'

tasks.title: Extend Session Schema for Forks
tasks.description: Add fork metadata to sessions table
tasks.technical_strategy: Add columns: parent_session_id (nullable FK), fork_point_message_id (nullable FK). Add index on parent_session_id for tree queries. Migration to add columns.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.db.repository import SessionsRepository; r = SessionsRepository(":memory:"); r.initialize(); print("OK")'

tasks.title: Implement Fork Operation
tasks.description: Create fork method in repository
tasks.technical_strategy: Copy session record with new ID. Copy messages up to fork_point. Set parent_session_id and fork_point_message_id. Allow provider/model override. Return new session ID.

tasks.title: Implement Fork UI
tasks.description: Add fork button and tree view
tasks.technical_strategy: Fork button in session header. Show fork dialog with model picker. Session sidebar shows tree structure with indentation. Fork marker in message history.

stories.title: Meta Self-Fix: File Edit Capability
stories.description: As an agent, I want to edit orchestrator source files so that I can fix bugs in myself
acceptance_criteria: Agent can locate orchestrator source directory
Write/patch tools work on orchestrator files

tasks.title: Implement Orchestrator Locator
tasks.description: Create utility to find orchestrator source
tasks.technical_strategy: Check ORCHESTRATOR_SOURCE_ROOT env var. Fallback to finding pyproject.toml with name='openexec-orchestration'. Cache result. Validate path exists and is writable.

tasks.title: Add Orchestrator Edit Approval Level
tasks.description: Create elevated approval for self-modification
tasks.technical_strategy: Add approval_level field to ApprovalRequest (NORMAL, ELEVATED, CRITICAL). Orchestrator file edits require ELEVATED. UI shows warning banner. Double-confirm for CRITICAL.

tasks.title: Implement Python Syntax Validator
tasks.description: Create validator for Python file edits
tasks.technical_strategy: Use ast.parse() to validate syntax before write. Use pyflakes for additional checks (undefined names). Return structured errors with line numbers. Reject invalid edits.

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Meta Edit
tasks.description: Write tests for orchestrator editing
tasks.technical_strategy: Use tmp directory as fake orchestrator source. Test locator with and without env var. Test elevated approval requirement. Test syntax validation rejection. Test backup creation.

stories.title: Meta Self-Fix: Build Capability
stories.description: As an agent, I want to build the orchestrator so that my changes can be tested
acceptance_criteria: Agent can trigger pip install -e . in orchestrator dir
stories.contract: OrchestratorBuilder: build() -> BuildResult(success: bool, output: str, test_results: Optional[TestResults])

tasks.title: Implement OrchestratorBuilder
tasks.description: Create builder with pip install -e
tasks.technical_strategy: Use run_shell_command with cwd=orchestrator_source. Run 'pip install -e .' in virtualenv. Capture stdout/stderr. Parse pip output for errors. Run pytest after successful install.

tasks.title: Implement Build Output Parser
tasks.description: Parse pip and pytest output
tasks.technical_strategy: Regex patterns for pip errors (Could not find, SyntaxError, ImportError). Parse pytest summary (passed, failed, errors). Extract failure details. Return structured BuildResult.
tasks.verification_script: python -c 'from openexec_orchestration.meta.parser import parse_build_output; print(parse_build_output("Successfully installed"))'

tasks.title: Unit Tests for Builder
tasks.description: Write tests with mocked subprocess
tasks.technical_strategy: Mock subprocess to return various pip/pytest outputs. Test success parsing, error extraction, test result parsing. Test with real orchestrator in CI (optional).

stories.title: Meta Self-Fix: Restart Request
stories.description: As an agent, I want to request orchestrator restart so that changes take effect
stories.contract: RestartManager: request_restart(reason: str) -> RestartRequest; execute_restart() -> None; is_restart_pending() -> bool

tasks.title: Implement RestartManager
tasks.description: Create restart coordination system
tasks.technical_strategy: Store restart request in DB with reason and requesting session. Emit event to UI for approval. On approval, save session state marker. Execute restart command from config (default: kill parent PID).

tasks.title: Implement Session Resume After Restart
tasks.description: Auto-resume sessions on startup
tasks.technical_strategy: On startup, check for pending_restart flag. Load session with flag. Send system message 'Restart completed. Resuming session.' Clear flag. Continue agent loop.

tasks.title: Create Restart Approval UI
tasks.description: Build UI for restart confirmation
tasks.technical_strategy: Modal with restart reason and warning. Show active sessions count. Require explicit 'Restart Now' button. Countdown timer (optional). Show restart progress indicator.

stories.title: Documentation: Conversational Orchestration
stories.description: As a developer, I want updated documentation so that I can understand and use conversational orchestration
acceptance_criteria: README updated with conversational mode section

tasks.title: Write Conversational Mode Documentation
tasks.description: Create main documentation for feature
tasks.technical_strategy: Structure: Overview, Quick Start, Architecture, API Reference, Examples, Troubleshooting. Use Mermaid for diagrams. Include code samples for common tasks.

tasks.title: Update README with Feature Overview
tasks.description: Add conversational orchestration to main README
tasks.technical_strategy: Add 'Conversational Mode' section after existing features. Include quick start command. Link to detailed docs. Add screenshot of UI.

tasks.title: Generate API Documentation
tasks.description: Create OpenAPI/Swagger docs
tasks.technical_strategy: Use FastAPI's auto-generated /docs. Export as openapi.json. Host alongside app. Add descriptions to all endpoints. Include example requests/responses.

stories.title: Goal Validation: End-to-End Testing
stories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests validating all primary goals so that I can verify the system works correctly
acceptance_criteria: E2E test creates session and sends message
E2E test executes tool with approval

tasks.title: Setup Playwright E2E Infrastructure
tasks.description: Configure Playwright for goal validation tests
tasks.technical_strategy: Create playwright.config.ts with goal-validation project. Setup test fixtures for session creation. Configure screenshot on failure. Use docker-compose for test environment.

tasks.title: E2E Test: Multi-Project Chat Sessions (G-001)
tasks.description: Validate session create/resume/fork
tasks.technical_strategy: Test: create session, send message, reload page, verify history. Test: fork session, verify both exist. Test: delete session, verify removed.

tasks.title: E2E Test: Provider-Agnostic Loop (G-002)
tasks.description: Validate provider switching
tasks.technical_strategy: Test: start with OpenAI, send message, switch to Anthropic, send message, verify both responses. Test: model picker shows correct availability.

tasks.title: E2E Test: Tool-Use with Approvals (G-003)
tasks.description: Validate tool execution and audit
tasks.technical_strategy: Test: trigger read_file (no approval), verify result. Test: trigger write_file, verify approval dialog, approve, verify file written. Test: audit log contains entries.

tasks.title: E2E Test: Auto-Context Injection (G-004)
tasks.description: Validate context appears in agent
tasks.technical_strategy: Test: create project with INTENT.md, start session, verify agent references intent content without explicit prompt. Test: modify tasks.json, verify agent sees update.

tasks.title: E2E Test: Meta Self-Fix (G-005)
tasks.description: Validate self-modification flow
tasks.technical_strategy: Test: ask agent to fix typo in orchestrator file (test file), verify elevated approval, verify build runs, verify restart requested. Use sandboxed orchestrator copy.
